definitions.text: The substance constituting Earth's atmosphere: a gaseous mixture of nitrogen, oxygen, and various trace gases.
examples: This drill runs on compressed air.
As you ascend, the air gets thinner.

definitions.text: The substance of the atmosphere seen as an agency of freshness.
examples: I'm going outside to get some air.
Open the window and let some air into the room.

definitions.text: One of the four elements of the ancient Greeks and Romans.
To bring (something) into contact with the air, so as to freshen or dry it.
examples: I'll hang these clothes on the rack to air them.

definitions.text: To let fresh air into (a room or a building), to ventilate.
examples: It's getting quite stuffy in this room: let's open the windows and air it.

definitions.text: To make public (an opinion, concern, issue, secret, differences, etc); to present to public view (and sometimes discussion).
examples: Residents used the public meeting to air their concerns about the poor state of the roads.
